stimulus: Researchers Sven Ahlqvist, Karina Boateng, and Diego Reyes report that while astronomers may have traditionally produced research papers within small national teams, the available data point to a steady shift in the opposite direction. ______blank astronomers are forming sprawling international collaborations—a trend made vivid by a sharp rise in the number of astronomy publications with author lists drawn from more than ten countries.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. "Increasingly" logically signals that the claim in this sentence—that astronomers are forming sprawling international collaborations—marks a change relative to the older small-team norm. As the previous sentence explains, available data point to a shift away from that norm. The claim describes the shift: a rise in international collaboration.

Choice A is incorrect because "similarly" illogically signals that the claim is parallel to, but separate from, the previous statement about the shift, when it actually elaborates on that shift. Choice B is incorrect because "for this reason" illogically signals that the rise in international collaboration is a result of the previous statement about the shift, when in fact the rise is the shift being described. Choice D is incorrect because "furthermore" illogically signals an additional, independent claim, when this sentence describes the very shift mentioned in the previous one.
